title: osteospermum imbricatum var. helichrysoides
intro: Osteospermum imbricatum var. helichrysoides, commonly known as the rock osteospermum, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and is believed to have various health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The therapeutic actions of Osteospermum imbricatum var. helichrysoides include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids. The bioactive constituents of this plant may also exhibit vasodilatory and hepatoprotective effects, making it a potential candidate for further research in the development of new medicinal compounds.

side_effects: Osteospermum imbricatum var. helichrysoides can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight, which may lead to conditions like photodermatitis or sunburn.
